message:
feat(demo_showcase): implement animation control API (bd-2szb)

Add canonical animation control methods to App:
- use_animations() - source of truth for animation decisions
- toggle_animations() - runtime toggle (used by AppMsg::ToggleAnimations)
- set_animations() - for tests with deterministic rendering

Also enhanced AppConfig.animations documentation and added 5 tests.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"bd-2qq7","title":"Wizard: integrate with demo data + trigger jobs/deployments","description":"Wire wizard completion into the demo backend.

Implementation notes:
- Use the shared domain action API (`demo_showcase data: action API …`) so the Wizard and Jobs page don't depend on each other.

Examples:
- Starting a deployment creates a new Deployment + associated Job.
- Emits log lines and toasts.

Acceptance:
- Finishing the wizard immediately changes Dashboard/Jobs/Logs content.
- Behavior is covered by unit tests at the action-API layer.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:11:03.497458412Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T22:30:56.227088421Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["data","demo_showcase","forms"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2qq7","depends_on_id":"bd-1qwj","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:19:55.728413037Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-2qq7","depends_on_id":"bd-28l3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:19:50.039176856Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-2qq7","depends_on_id":"bd-2omz","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:11:03.519336622Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-2qq7","depends_on_id":"bd-3pm6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:30:47.884659245Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-2qq7","depends_on_id":"bd-y3c8","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:19:52.897573354Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-2qx1","title":"demo_showcase page: Logs (styled + filterable)","description":"A live log viewer page that feels like a production-grade log tailer.

Why:
- Logging is where real apps live and die; this is the most convincing place to show `charmed_log` styling + ergonomics.
- It also provides the “ground truth” for cross-page workflows: Jobs should link to logs; errors should explain themselves.

Must showcase:
- `charmed_log`-styled output:
  - level coloring (trace/debug/info/warn/error)
  - structured fields (service, job_id, request_id, duration, user)
  - readable formatting even at narrow widths
- Bubbles viewport:
  - smooth scrolling, jump to top/bottom
  - follow mode (“tail -f”) that pauses when the user scrolls up
  - optional line wrapping toggle
- Filtering/search:
  - filter by level and service
  - query search with match highlight + next/prev navigation
  - correlation filters (e.g., “show logs for selected job”)
- Actions:
  - copy selected range or last N lines
  - export to a file (with a clear artifact path) and show toast confirmation
  - clear/reset view (without breaking follow mode)

Data requirements:
- Log stream is deterministic in test mode and correlated with the Jobs simulation.
- Includes occasional warnings/errors so filtering and drill-down are meaningful.

Acceptance:
- Looks and feels like a real log tailer (usable, not flashy-for-flashy’s-sake).
- Filtering is instant and predictable; follow mode never fights the user.

Testing:
- Unit tests:
  - filter + search correctness (stable ordering, edge cases)
  - follow mode logic (scroll up pauses, scroll bottom resumes)
  - export/copy behavior produces expected artifacts/messages
- E2E:
  - wizard -> job -> logs correlation scenario
  - logs copy/export/clear scenario (verifies artifacts + JSONL logs)

Notes:
- Avoid “fake rainbow logs”; prioritize legibility and density.
- Ensure `--no-color` / ASCII modes still produce clear output.","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:09:22.489282051Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T23:41:56.936627Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["charmed_log","demo_showcase","logs","page"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2qx1","depends_on_id":"bd-2pzh","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:09:22.508975562Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-2s8s","title":"SSH mode: basic auth + middleware polish","description":"Optional but high-impact polish: demonstrate `wish` authentication + middleware patterns without adding real complexity.

Why:
- SSH apps are more compelling when they feel "deployable" (even if it’s just a demo).
- Auth + middleware is a signature part of Wish’s architecture.

What to implement (choose one minimal auth mechanism):
- Password auth for demo (env/flag configured), OR
- Public-key allowlist (a small allowlist file / embedded demo key), OR
- "Local only" banner + explicit opt-in flag for public bind

Middleware polish:
- Connection logging middleware:
  - log connect/disconnect, username, remote addr, session duration
- Nice-to-have:
  - idle timeout / keepalive
  - connection banner (app name, version, keybindings hint)

Constraints:
- Must be clearly labeled "demo" and safe by default.
- Must not block non-SSH mode builds (feature-gated behind SSH feature).

Acceptance:
- Demonstrates Wish middleware/auth patterns end-to-end.
- Misconfiguration yields a friendly error/log message (not a panic).","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:13:52.852345698Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T23:27:12.737175397Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo_showcase","ssh","wish"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2s8s","depends_on_id":"bd-3chy","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:13:52.880077406Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-2s8s","depends_on_id":"bd-3xs0","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:21:57.129489814Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

 {"id":"bd-2tl1","title":"demo_showcase core: Program options + terminal lifecycle","description":"Ensure the demo configures bubbletea runtime options intentionally.

Implementation notes:
- Drive behavior from `demo_showcase::Config` (single source of truth), not scattered env reads.

In main/program bootstrap:
- alternate screen on by default (Config override: `--no-alt-screen`)
- focus reporting enabled
- bracketed paste enabled
- mouse enabled by toggle (Config + in-app Settings)
- set window title

Also ensure:
- graceful exit leaves terminal restored
- handle Ctrl+C and QuitMsg cleanly

Acceptance:
- Repeated runs never leave the terminal in a broken raw mode.
- `--no-alt-screen` produces a usable experience and allows demonstrating bubbletea `println/printf`.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:14:41.147935817Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T02:01:17.393130573Z","closed_at":"2026-01-28T02:01:17.393066163Z","close_reason":"Implemented Program options: focus reporting, mouse support from Config, window title. All 144 tests pass.","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["bubbletea","demo_showcase"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2tl1","depends_on_id":"bd-2q95","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:28:39.779293987Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-2tl1","depends_on_id":"bd-3j9x","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:14:41.161925295Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-2tl1","depends_on_id":"bd-cakm","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:17:13.369647978Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-2ucj","title":"demo_showcase UX: visual design system + theme presets","description":"Define the visual language so the app feels like one product.

Specify:
- Spacing scale (padding/margins), border styles, rounded vs sharp rules.
- Color semantics (primary/success/warn/error/muted/border) mapped to lipgloss ColorSlots.
- Typography/weight usage (bold/faint), emphasis rules.
- Theme presets to ship (e.g., Dracula, Nord, Catppuccin Mocha, Light, ASCII/NoColor) + naming.

Acceptance:
- Concrete rules that can be encoded as shared style helpers and reused everywhere.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:07:06.814237235Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T00:33:44.210289534Z","closed_at":"2026-01-28T00:33:44.210207271Z","close_reason":"done","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo_showcase","lipgloss","theme","ux"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2ucj","depends_on_id":"bd-33mv","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:17:02.290972241Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-2ucj","depends_on_id":"bd-3kcq","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:07:06.837012355Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-2xlv","title":"demo_showcase theme: load custom theme from file + contrast checks","description":"Optional but compelling showcase: load a custom theme from disk and validate it.

Why:
- It proves the theming system isn’t hard-coded and can be user-configured.
- Contrast validation demonstrates a mature design approach (readability matters).

Requirements:
- CLI/Config:
  - support `--theme-file <path>` (takes precedence over preset theme)
- File format:
  - pick a single stable format (recommend: TOML or JSON) and document the schema
  - map file values into semantic tokens (not raw ad-hoc styling)
- Validation:
  - validate required fields are present
  - run contrast checks for key pairs (fg/bg, muted/bg, selection/bg)
  - surface warnings in About/Diagnostics and/or as a toast on load
- Failure handling:
  - invalid file should not crash; fall back to a safe preset and show a clear error

Testability:
- Add unit tests for:
  - parsing valid theme files
  - rejecting invalid/missing fields
  - contrast warning behavior

Acceptance:
- Demonstrates lipgloss theme IO + validation in a real workflow.
- The app remains readable and stable even with bad user themes.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:08:27.588370671Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T23:31:46.886871699Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo_showcase","lipgloss"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2xlv","depends_on_id":"bd-2q95","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:28:43.964560626Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-2xlv","depends_on_id":"bd-39zu","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:08:27.601173399Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-2xlv","depends_on_id":"bd-3mh2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:17:40.699647818Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

 {"id":"bd-3gt6","title":"Quality: README section for demo_showcase","description":"Document how to run (and trust) the showcase.

Include:
- `cargo run -p demo_showcase` basics
- flags: theme/seed/no-animations/no-color/no-mouse/no-alt-screen, ssh mode, self-check
- keybindings summary + discoverability notes (help overlay, command palette if present)
- E2E instructions:
  - how to run the smoke tour and where artifacts/logs are written
  - how to run a single scenario for debugging

Also include a *feature coverage matrix* (self-contained):
- For each crate feature we claim (bubbletea/lipgloss/bubbles/glamour/huh/wish/charmed_log/harmonica), list:
  - where it appears in the UI (page/workflow)
  - which automated test(s) cover it (unit/e2e/snapshot)
  - any manual-only checks (mouse drag, SSH interactive)

Acceptance:
- Someone can run the demo and understand it without reading code.
- Someone can validate "everything works" without rediscovering the plan.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:13:31.297365816Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T22:59:00.527452079Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo_showcase","docs"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3gt6","depends_on_id":"bd-1i7e","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:21:31.956437980Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-3gt6","depends_on_id":"bd-1knu","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:21:34.482189415Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-3gt6","depends_on_id":"bd-2zeq","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:13:31.313191791Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-3if6","title":"Implement wish middleware pattern (logging, recover, ratelimit)","description":"Implement Wish middleware pattern (logging, recover, ratelimit) matching Go wish.

Scope:

- Implement standard middleware: logging, recover/panic handling, rate limiting.
- Ensure middleware order and error propagation match Go semantics.
- Add unit tests and integration tests using wish examples.","acceptance_criteria":"Criteria:
- Middleware API supports chaining and is used by default server builder.
- Logging/recover/ratelimit middleware behave as expected with tests.
- Integration test demonstrates middleware in a sample server.","notes":"Files: crates/wish/src/lib.rs (or middleware module), crates/wish/examples/*. Use legacy_wish for reference.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-01-25T01:09:28.687094198Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-26T01:47:14.493720286Z","closed_at":"2026-01-26T01:47:14.493694748Z","close_reason":"Middleware pattern already fully implemented: logging, recover, ratelimiter, accesscontrol, activeterm, elapsed, comment - all matching Go wish","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-3j9x","title":"demo_showcase: core TUI shell + router (bubbletea)","description":"Implement the reusable application shell that everything else plugs into.

Key ideas:
- One top-level App model that owns routing + global concerns (theme, status, notifications).
- Pages are sub-models/components; App delegates update/view and composes commands.
- Strong separation between domain data (fake backend) and presentation.

Acceptance:
- App boots into a multi-pane layout with navigation and at least one page.
- Clean message routing (no spaghetti downcasts everywhere).","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:06:04.314919933Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T22:06:04.333853721Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["architecture","bubbletea","demo_showcase"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3j9x","depends_on_id":"bd-yqh4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:06:04.333820659Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
-{"id":"bd-3jg2","title":"Interactions: bracketed paste support in inputs/search","description":"Bubbletea delivers paste sequences as `KeyMsg` with `paste=true` (and/or via terminal paste events).

Why:
- Paste is a "real app" interaction: users paste queries, IDs, paths, and commit hashes.
- Terminals can deliver large paste payloads; naïve handling can lag or break inputs.

Ensure paste works sensibly in:
- Jobs query bar
- Logs query/filter input
- Docs search
- Wizard text fields (huh)
- Notes scratchpad (textarea)

Edge cases:
- Very large paste:
  - should not freeze the UI
  - inputs should apply paste in a bounded way (e.g., respect char limits)
- Multi-line paste:
  - for search inputs: collapse whitespace/newlines into spaces (or clearly defined behavior)
  - for textarea: preserve newlines

Acceptance:
- Pasting a multi-word query "just works" across inputs.
- No hangs or panics with large paste payloads.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:12:37.767936662Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T23:28:53.947449304Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo_showcase","input","paste"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3jg2","depends_on_id":"bd-114p","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:12:37.786648712Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-3jg2","depends_on_id":"bd-2tl1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:17:58.203351507Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

Acceptance:

- No hangs or panics with large paste payloads.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:12:37.767936662Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T03:18:16.372890345Z","closed_at":"2026-01-28T03:18:16.372758850Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo_showcase","input","paste"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3jg2","depends_on_id":"bd-114p","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:12:37.786648712Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-3jg2","depends_on_id":"bd-2tl1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:17:58.203351507Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-3ju1","title":"charmed_log: Thread Safety & Reliability Hardening","description":"# Epic: charmed_log Thread Safety & Reliability Hardening

## Background
The charmed_log crate provides structured logging with lipgloss styling. During deep review,
several critical issues were identified that can cause application crashes or silent data loss.

## Critical Issues Identified

### 1. Lock Poisoning on RwLock::unwrap() - CRITICAL
**Location**: Lines 427, 468, 475, 481, 488, 494, 500, 506, 512, 518, 528, 579, 597
**Problem**: Every read/write lock acquisition uses .unwrap() which panics if lock is poisoned.
Lock poisoning occurs when a thread panics while holding a lock.
**Impact**: A single panic in any formatting routine breaks ALL future logging in the application.
**Risk**: Production reliability - one panic → application crash cascade

### 2. Silent I/O Error Discarding - CRITICAL
**Location**: Line 598: `let _ = inner.writer.write_all(output.as_bytes());`
**Problem**: Write failures to stderr go completely unnoticed (disk full, permission denied, pipe closed).
**Impact**: Logs silently disappear without any notification.
**Risk**: Silent data loss in production - critical audit logs may vanish

### 3. Backtrace Overhead in Caller Reporting - HIGH
**Location**: Line 308: `let bt = Backtrace::new();`
**Problem**: Every log call with report_caller=true captures and parses full stack trace.
**Impact**: 100x+ slower than regular logging when caller reporting is enabled.
**Risk**: Severe performance degradation if accidentally enabled in production

### 4. Double Lock Pattern Race Window - MEDIUM
**Location**: Lines 579-598
**Problem**: log() acquires read lock, formats, drops, then acquires write lock.
Configuration could change between the two lock acquisitions.
**Impact**: Possible inconsistency in output formatting under concurrent configuration changes.

## Design Philosophy for Fixes
- Logging should be resilient - never crash the application
- Errors should be reported through a configurable error handler
- Performance-critical paths should have explicit opt-in for expensive features
- Thread safety should use recover-from-poisoning strategy for logging

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All lock acquisitions handle poisoning gracefully
- [ ] I/O errors are propagated to configurable error handler
- [ ] Backtrace overhead is documented with performance warning
- [ ] All changes have regression tests
- [ ] Zero clippy warnings","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-01-28T02:15:10.326116387Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T03:00:22.270057599Z","closed_at":"2026-01-28T03:00:22.269990013Z","close_reason":"All child beads complete: bd-3lwk (error handler), bd-1bja (lock poisoning), bd-2go9 (caller overhead docs), bd-3lk7 (double-lock fix). charmed_log now has robust thread safety and reliability.","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["charmed_log","critical","thread-safety"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3ju1","depends_on_id":"bd-gyzr","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-28T02:15:10.342713910Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-3kcq","title":"demo_showcase: product concept + UX + visual spec","description":"Define the *product story* and UX blueprint for the demo_showcase app so the implementation is cohesive (not a grab-bag of components).

Deliverables (in-beads, not a separate doc):
- App concept (what it is pretending to be), core user journeys, navigation IA.
- Keybindings + mouse interactions philosophy (keyboard-first, mouse-supported).
- Visual system: spacing scale, typography/weight usage, borders, accent colors, status semantics.
- Accessibility/readability constraints: contrast, focus states, "NO_COLOR"/ASCII fallbacks.

Acceptance:
- All subsequent implementation issues can reference this epic for the intended "feel" and UX rules.","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:06:02.355567902Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T22:06:02.371452730Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo_showcase","design","ux"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3kcq","depends_on_id":"bd-yqh4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:06:02.371406974Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-3kk5","title":"Docs: implement markdown viewer (glamour + viewport)","description":"Implement the Docs page viewer UI: beautiful Markdown rendering in a real app chrome.

Why:
- The Docs page is a flagship glamour showcase and doubles as onboarding.
- It must look great *and* behave like a real reader (fast, searchable, resizable).

Requirements:
- Render markdown with glamour using:
  - current theme
  - current content width
  - syntax/line-number toggles (if enabled)
- Display rendered output inside `bubbles::viewport`.
- Scrolling:
  - smooth line/page scrolling
  - clear scroll position indication (optional)
- Resize:
  - re-render on width changes (avoid re-render loops)

Performance considerations:
- Cache the last rendered output per doc + width + theme/toggles to avoid unnecessary work.

Acceptance:
- Markdown looks great and is readable; scrolling is smooth.

 {"id":"bd-3pm6","title":"demo_showcase data: action API (start job/deploy, emit logs/toasts)","description":"Create a clean *domain action API* so multiple UI surfaces can trigger the same behaviors without depending on each other.

Rationale:
- Currently Jobs page actions and Wizard completion both need to start jobs/deployments.
- If Wizard depends on Jobs page code (or vice versa), the architecture becomes tangled.

Deliverables:
- A set of pure-ish functions (or a small service struct) that:
  - create/start/cancel/retry jobs
  - start deployments / rollbacks
  - emit structured log entries
  - emit user-facing notifications (as app messages)
- The action layer should accept the current domain state + inputs and return:
  - updated state
  - side effects expressed as bubbletea `Cmd` / `AsyncCmd` (feature-gated)
  - events/messages for UI (toasts, navigation)

Implementation guidance:
- Prefer composing effects with `bubbletea::batch(...)` (toast + log + state refresh) instead of ad-hoc "do one thing" commands.
- Use `bubbletea::sequence(...)` for any multi-step workflow that must occur in order (e.g., release-terminal -> run -> restore, or staged deploy steps).

Acceptance:
- Jobs page and Wizard both call this API (no cross-dependencies between pages).
- The API is unit-testable (given state + input -> expected state/events).","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:30:09.840599209Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T01:13:41.700985132Z","closed_at":"2026-01-28T01:13:41.700919670Z","close_reason":"done","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["architecture","data","demo_showcase"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3pm6","depends_on_id":"bd-278q","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:30:09.860556382Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-3pm6","depends_on_id":"bd-2hwq","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:30:28.941199473Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
-{"id":"bd-3ps4","title":"Interactions: enable mouse mode + click/scroll mapping","description":"Enable mouse support in `demo_showcase` and map mouse events to a coherent UX (scroll, click-to-focus/select).

Why:
- Mouse support is a premium signal and makes the showcase feel like a real product.
- It also validates bubbletea’s mouse plumbing (`MouseMsg` parsing + crossterm integration).

Implementation requirements:
- Program configuration:
  - run with mouse enabled (prefer `with_mouse_cell_motion()` to support hover/cell-motion where useful).
  - ensure mouse can be disabled via Settings/Config toggle.
- Scroll mapping:
  - wheel scroll should apply to the *focused pane* (viewport, list, table, file preview).
  - when nothing is explicitly focused, default to the primary content pane.
- Click mapping:
  - click selects within lists/tables/filepicker when coordinates hit rows.
  - click on dashboard cards triggers selection/drill-down.
  - click-to-focus inputs (search bars, text inputs, etc.).

Behavioral constraints:
- Mouse must not fight keyboard navigation:
  - keyboard remains first-class, mouse is additive.
  - selection/focus updates should be consistent regardless of input source.
- Headless/CI safety:
  - mouse support should be a no-op when renderer/terminal features are unavailable.

Acceptance:
- Mouse scroll feels natural in viewports/lists/tables/file preview.
- Clicking to select/focus works in the major screens.
- Disabling mouse immediately stops mouse-driven changes.

Testing:
- Unit tests:
  - given `MouseMsg` wheel events, verify the correct component state changes (scroll offset, selection).
  - click hit-testing logic for at least one list/table and one card grid.
- E2E (optional but preferred):
  - use the harness “raw byte” mode to inject xterm mouse sequences and assert state changes in artifacts.

Notes:
- Keep hit-testing deterministic: base calculations on known layout metrics (width/height + fixed styles).","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:12:32.064610623Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T23:43:29.882924534Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo_showcase","input","mouse"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3ps4","depends_on_id":"bd-114p","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:12:32.093132249Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-3ps4","depends_on_id":"bd-28jp","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:17:56.981806786Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-3ps4","depends_on_id":"bd-2tl1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:17:55.722943138Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

  - when nothing is explicitly focused, default to the primary content pane.

  - click selects within lists/tables/filepicker when coordinates hit rows.

- Mouse must not fight keyboard navigation:

- Headless/CI safety:

- Keep hit-testing deterministic: base calculations on known layout metrics (width/height + fixed styles).","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:12:32.064610623Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T03:18:49.256919092Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo_showcase","input","mouse"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3ps4","depends_on_id":"bd-114p","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:12:32.093132249Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-3ps4","depends_on_id":"bd-28jp","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:17:56.981806786Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-3ps4","depends_on_id":"bd-2tl1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:17:55.722943138Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

 {"id":"bd-3rwl","title":"Jobs: implement jobs table/list + selection","description":"Implement the main Jobs/Tasks page: a dense, usable jobs table/list with selection.

Why:
- This is the core "ops console" workflow: see what’s running, what failed, and drill into details.
- It showcases Bubbles components (table/list, paginator, progress, spinner) working together.

Implementation options:
- Use `bubbles::table` for a dense table view, or
- Use `bubbles::list` for a richer list with descriptions.

Data to show (minimum):
- job name / id
- status (running/succeeded/failed/cancelled)
- progress (when applicable)
- duration / started-at
- owner/service/environment (as fits the domain model)

Interactions:
- Keyboard navigation and selection.
- Selection updates a details pane (separate task) and enables actions (start/cancel/retry).

Acceptance:
- Keyboard navigation works and feels consistent.
- Selection is stable across refreshes and filtering changes (best-effort).","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:10:18.314504691Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T00:58:13.418655253Z","closed_at":"2026-01-28T00:58:13.418589240Z","close_reason":"done","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["bubbles","demo_showcase","jobs"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3rwl","depends_on_id":"bd-1dr6","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:10:18.330182628Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-3rwl","depends_on_id":"bd-2k35","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:18:31.091452200Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-3rwl","depends_on_id":"bd-3bz7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:18:35.920756563Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-3rwl","depends_on_id":"bd-7jxu","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:18:33.415669784Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
